title: Dryopteris pleiosoros
taxonomy: Dryopteris pleiosoros is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a vascular plant that belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The species is classified as a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. At the rank of genus, it is classified under Dryopteris, a genus of wood ferns. Dryopteris pleiosoros is further classified at the rank of species, with its full classification being Dryopteris pleiosoros.

intro: Dryopteris pleiosoros, also known as the Japanese wood fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Dryopteris, characterized by its distinctive frond structure and ability to thrive in shaded, woodland environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and oxidative stress, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Dryopteris pleiosoros include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in combination with other herbs to create infusions and topical creams for the treatment of various health conditions.
